PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT

**Group Life Insurer Member Portal**

Master Policyholder, Member, and Annuitant Self-Service Platform

Claims • Endorsements • Renewals • Member Add/Remove via LWD • Billing • Documents • Reporting

| Attribute | Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Version** | v3.1 (multi-stakeholder exhaustive PRD baseline) |
| **Date** | 31 March 2026 |
| **Status** | Revised execution baseline |
| **Design basis** | v2.0 combined PRD \+ CTO assessment closure \+ explicit API/event/service-boundary execution detail |
| **Primary user** | Master policyholder administrator, HR/payroll operations, approver, insurer operations, optional member / annuitant |
| **Business lines covered** | GTL, group credit life, group savings, group ULIP, gratuity / superannuation, annuity, pension |
| **Core servicing journeys** | Add member, remove member with LWD, claim initiation, endorsement management, renewal, billing, documents, reports |
| Revision intent | Preserve the stronger execution-grade PRD baseline, then add deeper service boundaries, canonical API and event contracts, state-transition guards, test strategy, and sprint-ready delivery sequencing.v3.1 addition: expands the PRD into a true multi-stakeholder group portal covering MPH, broker/channel/intermediary, TPA/service partner, member/annuitant and insurer operations workspaces, including wallet/cash-deposit logic, corporate and bank-document handling, business MIS, partner earnings/loan data views, service tracking, and granular document upload/view permissions. |

| Document intentThis v3.0 revision closes the assessment feedback by retaining the stronger combined PRD baseline and expanding the execution-critical layers: target architecture, service boundaries, canonical API contracts, asynchronous event catalog, entity-specific state guards, delivery work packages, and go-live readiness. |
| :---- |

**Section Map**

**1\.** Executive summary and product objectives

**2\.** Design basis and source synthesis

**3\.** Scope, principles, and success metrics

**4\.** Information architecture and role model

**5\.** Detailed screen requirements by module

**6\.** Transaction parameter models and validations

**7\.** Business-line variants across GTL, GCL, ULIP, savings, annuity, pension

**8\.** Functional requirements, UX requirements, and state models

**9\.** Data model, integrations, APIs, events, and documents

**10\.** Security, compliance, audit, analytics, non-functional requirements, and delivery phasing

**1\. Executive Summary**

This PRD defines the target-state member portal for a group life insurer where the master policyholder is the primary operational actor, while insured members and annuitants may also receive controlled self-service capabilities for selected product lines. The portal is not a thin customer-service skin. It is a controlled servicing platform for membership administration, claims initiation, endorsements, renewals, billing visibility, document access, approvals, and audit.

The design must work across group term and adjacent retirement / savings lines without forcing the insurer to maintain separate portals for each line of business. The common shell remains stable, while product-line tabs, validations, forms, and downstream integrations vary by scheme configuration.

The most important design decision is that member exit is modeled as a business event, not a deletion. The Last Working Day (LWD) or equivalent exit date becomes the anchor for coverage cessation, financial impact, eligibility checks, claim blocking rules, and audit history.

| Core design principle One login, one portfolio, one policy-aware shell for all group life servicing. Every user action must be attributable to a named actor, tied to a policy and scheme context, effective-dated, statused, and replayable through durable audit history. |
| :---- |

**2\. What This Version Improves**

| Theme | How v2.0 strengthens the PRD |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Combined document quality** | Merges the stronger portal-shell structure of the MPH PRD, the broader screen inventory of the member portal PRD, and the stricter field-level operational controls from the detailed requirements note. |
| **Broader line coverage** | Makes GTL, GCL, group savings, ULIP, gratuity, superannuation, annuity, and pension variants explicit instead of treating them as minor extensions. |
| **Exact screen baseline** | Defines a complete navigation shell, module-wise screens, primary widgets, actions, validations, and role visibility, plus deep field models for the high-risk transactional journeys. |
| **Operational control** | Strengthens maker-checker, segregation of duties, status models, query/response loops, auditability, notification logic, and partial-bulk acceptance behavior. |
| **Regulatory readiness** | Explicitly incorporates insured-register integrity, LWD-based cessation, nomination and bank-data handling, claims SLA timers, GST display impacts, privacy, and cyber logging expectations. |
| **Implementation readiness** | Adds entity model, API surface, event catalog, document templates, analytics, phased delivery, and open design decisions so engineering and product can take the PRD into implementation. |

**3\. Design Basis and Source Synthesis**

**•** AXA Essential UC1 PRD v1.1 is used as the packaging and decisioning baseline: requirement IDs, product objectives, controlled workflows, auditability, and implementation-grade structure.

**•** Group\_Life\_MPH\_Portal\_PRD\_v1 contributes the strongest portal-shell framing: modular information architecture, screen-level policies, role matrix, and module-specific detail.

**•** Group\_Life\_Member\_Portal\_PRD\_v1 contributes the broadest screen inventory, transaction parameter models, business-line variants, state model, and functional requirement framing.

**•** Member Portal Requirements for Group Life Master Policyholder contributes the strongest regulatory and field-level logic for claims, endorsements, renewals, LWD exits, GST handling, CKYC/KYC, and event-specific validations.

**•** Current insurer/admin patterns confirm that sponsor/admin portals commonly support enrolment, member update, termination or reinstatement, coverage summary, statements, forms, claims support, and reports; group annuity lines often have separate annuitant access patterns.

| Important interpretation There is no single industry-standard 'exact screen requirement' document for group life portals. This PRD is therefore an exact target-state design baseline synthesized from the uploaded drafts and current market/regulatory patterns, not a claim that one insurer has published a universal canonical UI. |
| :---- |

**4\. Business Context**

Group life and retirement administration remains operationally heavy. HR teams, payroll teams, trustees, lender operations, brokers, and insurer back offices exchange spreadsheets, forms, certificates, and claim documents across repeated servicing cycles. The portal must absorb those recurring journeys without exposing raw PAS complexity, while still preserving insurer-grade controls.

The portal must support both employer-employee and non-employer-employee constructs where permitted, including lender-borrower variants. That means it needs configurable entry/exit rules, scheme-specific effective-date controls, claim-payee rules, product-line document checklists, and controlled approval thresholds.

**5\. Product Objectives and Success Metrics**

| ID | Objective |
| :---- | :---- |
| OBJ-01 | Enable master policyholders to self-serve routine policy servicing without email-driven operations. |
| OBJ-02 | Provide one portfolio shell across all group life and retirement lines with policy-aware context switching. |
| OBJ-03 | Reduce endorsement, claim-intimation, and renewal preparation turnaround through structured workflows and guided validations. |
| OBJ-04 | Preserve insurer controls through maker-checker, effective dating, approval thresholds, data lineage, and complete audit history. |
| OBJ-05 | Support both single-record and bulk journeys for member administration and renewal census handling. |
| OBJ-06 | Provide high-confidence operational views for premiums, fund values, statements, payout history, claims, and documents. |

| Metric | Target |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Portal adoption** | \>90% of routine endorsements and membership changes initiated in portal within 6 months |
| **Claim-intimation digitization** | \>80% of eligible group claims started through portal or portal-assisted intake |
| **Single-record journey time** | \<10 minutes for add member, remove member, and standard service requests |
| **Bulk journey efficiency** | Partial acceptance with annotated error output for bulk census / member submissions |
| **Audit retrieval time** | \<1 business day to answer who changed what, when, and under which approval |
| **Renewal readiness** | 100% of policies approaching renewal visible with tasks, notices, and census status |

**6\. Users and Role Model**

| Role | Typical user | Primary capabilities | Key constraint |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Master Policyholder Super Admin** | Head HR / benefits admin / trustee admin | Full portfolio access, user setup, approvals, high-privilege servicing, reporting | Cannot approve own submission |
| **Master Policyholder Maker** | HR ops / payroll ops / scheme operator | Create drafts for member, endorsement, claim, renewal, service requests | Approval required where configured |
| **Master Policyholder Approver** | Authorized signatory / finance / controller | Approve and submit eligible items; see financial impact | Must be distinct from maker; MFA re-auth |
| **Viewer / Auditor** | Management / audit | Read-only dashboards, reports, history, documents | No write actions |
| **Member** | Insured member | Optional access to own coverage, certificates, nominee/KYC/bank tasks | No sponsor-wide visibility |
| **Annuitant / Pensioner** | Retiree / annuitant | Payout history, tax certificates, existence certificate, bank/address tasks | No sponsor-wide visibility |
| **Insurer Operations** | Back office / servicing / underwriting / claims ops | Review, approve, query, reject, complete downstream servicing | Subject to internal role scoping |
| **Broker / Intermediary (optional)** | Broker servicing team | Restricted portfolio status and renewal support | Sensitive fields masked unless explicitly allowed |

**7\. Scope**

**7.1 In Scope**

**•** Authentication, MFA / OTP, first-time activation, user invitation, password reset, and session control.

**•** Policy portfolio dashboard and policy-aware servicing home across all covered business lines.

**•** Member roster, member detail, add member, update member, remove member via LWD, and bulk member operations.

**•** Endorsement cockpit for member additions and exits, corrections, coverage changes, salary/category movements, nominee or beneficiary changes, rider changes, and permitted fund operations.

**•** Claims initiation, dynamic document checklist, tracking timeline, query handling, and payout-instruction capture.

**•** Renewal calendar, renewal detail, census upload and lock, coverage confirmation, quote review, and acceptance.

**•** Billing and statements, premium and tax breakup, remittance references, reconciliation status, fund / contribution views where relevant.

**•** Document center, certificate viewer, communication history, forms, downloadable reports, tasks, and service requests.

**•** Administration, RBAC, audit trail, notifications, grievances, and compliance-facing history.

**7.2 Out of Scope for Release 1**

**•** New quote purchase or proposal generation for fresh group business.

**•** Full claim adjudication decisioning and settlement execution inside the portal; these remain in claims core or insurer workbench.

**•** Full payments gateway and collections orchestration beyond visibility, remittance references, and reconciliation status.

**•** Open-market annuity purchase execution and full financial-planning experiences.

**•** Standalone broker CRM.

**8\. Product Principles**

| Principle | Portal implication |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Policy-first navigation** | Every workflow is anchored to a policy and scheme context; the user should never be unsure which contract is being serviced. |
| **Transaction-led UX** | Claims, endorsements, renewals, and membership changes use guided wizards, document collection, and explicit review steps. |
| **LWD as a first-class event** | Remove-member journeys capture LWD, derive coverage cease treatment, and compute financial or fund impact instead of hard deleting records. |
| **Effective-dated servicing** | Every material change captures request date, effective date, decision date, actor, and before/after state. |
| **One shell, many product variants** | Common navigation is stable; fields, tabs, and validations vary by product line and scheme configuration. |
| **Audit by design** | All actions, document uploads, approvals, rejections, queries, downloads, and field changes are timestamped and replayable. |
| **Operational empathy** | Support bulk upload, partial acceptance, clear error messages, save-as-draft, annotated exports, and self-explanatory statuses. |

**9\. Information Architecture**

| Navigation module | Primary screens / intent |
| :---- | :---- |
| Home / Dashboard | Portfolio summary, alerts, tasks, KPIs, recent activity, renewals due |
| Policies | Policy list, detail, benefit summary, product-line tabs, servicing actions |
| Members | Roster, member detail, add/update/remove, nominee, bulk operations |
| Endorsements | Create endorsement, tracker, review, compare, approval, detail timeline |
| Claims | Claim list, initiation wizard, document upload, claim detail, payout instruction |
| Renewals | Renewal calendar, detail, census upload, quote comparison, acceptance |
| Billing & Statements | Statements, premium breakup, tax display, remittance references, reconciliation |
| Documents | Certificates, schedules, claim kits, statements, renewal packs, tax certificates |
| Reports | Standard reports, scheduled exports, ad hoc report templates |
| Tasks & Notifications | Pending approvals, insurer queries, SLA-risk items, reminders |
| Service Requests | General servicing requests, grievances, assistance workflows |
| Administration | Users, roles, access scopes, audit, configuration references |

**10\. Portfolio and Product-Line Coverage**

| Business line | Additional portal expectations |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Group Term Life (GTL)** | Coverage amount, salary-linked cover, riders, death/disability claims, certificate issuance, LWD-driven exits |
| **Group Credit Life (GCL)** | Borrower roster, loan number, outstanding balance, assignment or authorization logic, lender-linked claim split |
| **Group Savings / Gratuity / Superannuation** | Contribution history, fund balance, vesting, exits, retirement, withdrawal, benefit settlement |
| **Group ULIP** | Units, NAV, fund value, allocation status, switch/redirection if allowed, unit-linked benefit execution |
| **Group Annuity** | Annuitant detail, payout frequency, payout advice, bank servicing, existence certificate, tax certificates |
| **Group Pension** | Retirement corpus, vesting milestones, annuity settlement status, pension servicing, retiree records |

**11\. Screen Inventory**

| Module | Primary screens |
| :---- | :---- |
| Authentication | Login, MFA / OTP, first-time activation, forgot password, invitation acceptance, session risk challenge |
| Home | Portfolio dashboard, action center, alerts & notices, recent transactions, operational KPIs |
| Policies | Policy list, policy detail, benefits / plans, product extension tabs, servicing action rail |
| Members | Member list, member detail, add member wizard, update member wizard, remove member / LWD wizard, bulk upload, nominee screen |
| Endorsements | Endorsement list, create endorsement, review / compare, approval, detail timeline, insurer query response |
| Claims | Claim list, claim initiation wizard, event details, documents, payout instruction, claim detail / timeline |
| Renewals | Renewal calendar, renewal detail, renewal inputs, census upload, quote review, acceptance |
| Billing | Statement list, statement detail, remittance upload, reconciliation view, fund / contribution view |
| Documents | Document center, certificate viewer, communication history, download pack |
| Reports | Reports home, standard reports, scheduled exports, ad hoc templates |
| Tasks & Notifications | Task inbox, notifications center, SLA aging, pending approvals |
| Administration | User and role administration, access scopes, audit trail, service requests, grievances |

**11.1 Authentication and Access Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Login** | Primary authentication into portal | User ID / email / mobile, password, policy/corporate ID, help links | Login, forgot password, alternate verification | Account lockout, password policy, trusted-device policy | All |
| **MFA / OTP Verification** | Step-up authentication for login and sensitive approvals | OTP entry, resend timer, alternate channel, trusted device | Verify, resend, change channel | OTP expiry, attempt limit, device trust | All / mandatory for approvers |
| **First-Time Activation** | New user activation from invite | Invite token, identity checks, password creation, terms acceptance | Activate account | Token expiry, password strength | Invited users only |
| **Forgot Password** | Credential recovery | Identity lookup, OTP/email verification, new password | Reset password | Cooldown, prior-password history | All |
| **Invitation Acceptance** | User accepts organization role | Organization preview, assigned policies, role preview, MFA setup | Accept invite, reject | Expiry, duplicate activation | Invited users only |

**11.2 Home and Dashboard Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Portfolio Dashboard** | Landing page across active policies | Policy cards, KPIs, quick actions, alerts | Open policy, launch workflow | Only policies in access scope shown | All |
| **Action Center** | Prioritized operational work | Pending approvals, insurer queries, incomplete drafts, SLA risk tasks | Open task, assign, remind | Task state / ownership checks | Maker, approver, ops |
| **Alerts & Notices** | Time-sensitive notifications | Renewals due, premium issues, claim updates, data quality notices | View details, dismiss, download | Severity, policy relevance | Scoped users |
| **Operational KPIs** | Summary metrics | Active lives, open claims, pending endorsements, outstanding statements | Drill down | Metric freshness and policy filter | All |

**11.3 Policy Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Policy List** | Search and select policy context | Search, filters, cards/table, statuses | Open policy, favorite, export | Access-scope checks | All |
| **Policy Detail** | Single policy servicing home | Header summary, product badges, dates, status, action rail | Start claim, add member, start renewal | Policy status and effective-date checks | All |
| **Benefit / Plan Summary** | View benefit structure | Plans, coverage slabs, riders, waiting periods, premium mode | Download schedule | Read-only from configured source | All |
| **Product Extension Tabs** | Specialized views by line | Fund values, annuity payout, loan-linked fields, vesting milestones | Line-specific actions | Product applicability | Scoped users |

**11.4 Member Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Member List** | Central roster with filters and bulk actions | Search, filters, export, status counts, action menu | Add, export, bulk remove, open detail | Status, scheme, access-scope filters | Maker+, viewer read-only |
| **Member Detail** | View one member record | Demographics, coverage, history, documents, claims, nominee | Edit, remove, raise claim, download certificate | Status and rights checks | Scoped users |
| **Add Member Wizard** | Single member onboarding | Multi-step member, coverage, nominee, docs, review | Save draft, submit | Entry-window rules, duplicate checks, mandatory fields | Maker / admin |
| **Update Member Wizard** | Controlled correction or change | Editable field groups, reason codes, effective date, compare before/after | Save, submit | Editable-field and effective-date rules | Maker / admin |
| **Remove Member / LWD Wizard** | Exit a member or schedule exit | Member search, LWD, exit reason, financial impact, docs, review | Save, submit | LWD, open-claim block, premium/fund logic | Maker / admin |
| **Bulk Member Upload** | Bulk add/update/remove | Template download, upload, validation results, partial accept, error export | Validate, submit batch | Template/version checks, chunking, approval | Maker / admin |
| **Beneficiary / Nominee Screen** | Manage nominee or beneficiary | Beneficiary list, shares, proof docs, effective dates | Add, modify, archive | Share totals, proof, maker-checker by rule | Scoped users |

**11.5 Endorsement Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Endorsement List** | Track all endorsements | Filters, status tabs, search, export | Open, withdraw draft, clone | Status-based actions | Scoped users |
| **Create Endorsement** | Select type and start request | Type picker, policy context, effective date, impacted record scope | Start draft | Type applicability and date rules | Maker / admin |
| **Endorsement Review** | Before/after compare and impact review | Compare panel, premium/fund impact, docs, declarations | Submit for approval | Mandatory impact visibility | Maker / admin |
| **Approval Screen** | Approver decisioning | Summary, impact, docs, comments, approve/reject | Approve, reject, return | SoD and re-auth | Approver only |
| **Endorsement Detail / Timeline** | Post-submission tracking | Timeline, remarks, documents, insurer queries, audit | Respond, download, withdraw if allowed | Lifecycle-based actions | Scoped users |

**11.6 Claims Screens**

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Claim List** | Track all claim cases | Tabs, search, filters, SLA timers, status | Start claim, open case, export | Policy, member, role filters | Scoped users |
| **Claim Initiation Wizard** | Capture new claim | Member search, claim type, event details, claimant, documents, review | Save draft, submit | Coverage-in-force, event-date, payee readiness | Maker / admin |
| **Claim Document Upload** | Upload and manage evidence | Checklist, upload control, virus-scan status, version history | Upload, replace, comment | Type / size / mandatory-doc rules | Scoped users |
| **Claim Detail / Timeline** | Case tracking after submission | Timeline, status, insurer remarks, pending tasks, communications | Respond to query, download summary | Status-based actions | Scoped users |
| **Payout Instruction View** | Validate payee and bank details | Payee type, bank details, mandate docs, assignment data | Confirm or update | Payee identity rules, bank validation | Scoped users / insurer ops |

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Renewal Calendar** | Upcoming expiries and renewal readiness | Policy cards, countdowns, status, tasks | Open renewal, export | Window visibility | Scoped users |
| **Renewal Detail** | Single policy renewal workspace | Current terms, prior-year stats, tasks, notices | Start census prep, request quote | Policy status and access scope | Maker+ / ops |
| **Renewal Inputs** | Capture assumptions and coverage choices | Renewal period, benefits, payment mode, assumptions | Save, proceed | ARD and product-option checks | Maker / admin |
| **Census Upload \+ Validation** | Prepare renewal dataset | Upload, validation report, exception grid, lock action | Validate, lock census | Template, duplicate, DOB/DOJ/LWD checks | Maker / approver |
| **Quote Review / Acceptance** | Approve and accept renewal commercial terms | Premium comparison, GST, terms, documents, approval | Accept, reject, query | Approver role, SoD, quote validity | Approver / admin |

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Statement List** | View billing history | Filters, statement summary, download links | Open, export | Policy scope | All scoped users |
| **Statement Detail** | View one statement | Premium, tax, charges, due date, remittance refs | Download, mark remittance sent | Statement lifecycle rules | Scoped users |

| **Certificate Viewer** | View COI or certificate | Inline preview, metadata, version history | Download, share secure link if allowed | Version and scope checks | Scoped users |

| Screen | Purpose | Primary widgets | Primary actions | Key validations / controls | Roles |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Task Inbox** | Operational work queue | Tasks, owner, aging, status, severity | Open, claim, escalate | Assignment rules | Scoped users |
| **Notification Center** | Central communications hub | Unread/read items, categories, actions | Open, dismiss | Per-user preferences | All |
| **Reports Home** | Access reporting | Standard reports, schedules, ad hoc templates | Run, schedule, export | Data-scope enforcement | Scoped users |
| **User & Role Administration** | Manage organization access | Users, roles, policies, locations, permissions | Invite, edit, disable | Admin-only, SoD rules | Super admin only |
| **Audit Trail** | Review immutable history | Search, filters, timeline, actor, before/after | Export evidence | Immutable and filterable | Scoped admins / audit |
| **Service Requests / Grievances** | Handle non-standard servicing or complaints | Case form, category, attachments, status | Submit, track | Mandatory fields, SLA classification | Scoped users |

**12\. Transaction Parameter Models**

**12.1 Common Policy Context Parameters**

| Parameter group | Fields | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Policy / scheme context** | Policy number, scheme code, policyholder org, line of business, segment, channel | Mandatory on all transactions; default from current policy context |
| **Contract dates** | Commencement date, expiry date, annual renewal date, grace rules, next premium due date | Derived from PAS or policy core; used for effective-date controls |
| **Commercial context** | Premium mode, tax regime, charge set, billing status, arrears or outstanding amount | Read-only at start; affects financial impact screens |
| **Access context** | User role, org scope, policy scope, location scope, maker-checker requirement | Derived from IAM and policy assignment |
| **Document context** | Required forms, certificate template, declaration template, communication templates | Driven by product / scheme / workflow |

**12.2 Add Member Parameters**

| Parameter group | Fields | Key controls |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Identity** | Employee/member ID, title, name, DOB, gender, relationship, government IDs, CKYC identifier if enabled | Duplicate check, age rules, ID format rules |
| **Employment / source relationship** | DOJ / join date, location, department, grade, class, payroll code, lender/borrower refs where applicable | Entry windows and class rules |
| **Coverage** | Plan, benefit option, sum assured / salary basis, riders, contribution or premium, coverage effective date | Scheme and product validations |
| **Nominee / beneficiary** | Name, relation, share %, DOB where needed, contact, bank details if required at setup | Share-total and proof validations |
| **Documents** | KYC, authorization, onboarding proof, declaration, salary or employment proof if required | Dynamic checklist by scheme |
| **System outputs** | Acknowledgement no., validation result, premium estimate, certificate trigger, status | Generated after submission or downstream approval |

**12.3 Remove Member via LWD Parameters**

| Parameter group | Fields | Key controls |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Member selection** | Member ID, policy context, current status, open claims flag, active cover flag | Cannot proceed on invalid / incompatible statuses |
| **Exit data** | Termination type, LWD / exit date, cease date, future-scheduled exit flag, remarks | LWD mandatory; cannot predate join date |
| **Financial impact** | Refund premium eligible, refund amount, tax treatment, recoveries, fund-impact summary | Computed or returned by rating/fund services |
| **Documentary support** | Exit letter, HR letter, resignation / retirement evidence, authorization | Optional or mandatory by scheme |
| **Approvals** | Maker, approver, approver timestamp, insurer reviewer | SoD enforced |

**12.4 Claim Parameters**

| Parameter group | Fields | Key controls |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Member and coverage check** | Member, certificate no., cover in force on event date, waiting period result, exclusion flags | Validated before claim can proceed |
| **Event** | Claim type, date of event, place, cause category, hospitalisation, police case, retirement/withdrawal mode | Dynamic by claim type and product |
| **Claimant / payee** | Claimant identity, relationship, nominee / beneficiary, assignment or lender authorization, bank details, NEFT mandate | Payee controls vary by scheme |
| **Documents** | Death certificate, medical proof, police papers, retirement letter, loan statement, annuity proof, discharge forms | Dynamic checklist and no piece-meal requests goal |
| **Workflow** | Claim reference, submission date, SLA timers, insurer query count, status, last action date | Timeline must remain visible end to end |

**12.5 Renewal Parameters**

| Parameter group | Fields | Key controls |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Policy cycle** | Current expiry, renewal effective date, notice issued date, quote validity | Aligned to ARD and renewal window rules |
| **Population / census** | Current lives, joiners, leavers, pending changes, locked census version | Immutable versioning after lock |
| **Coverage and assumptions** | Selected benefits, riders, salary bands, premium mode, underwriting assumptions | Line and scheme controls |
| **Commercials** | Current premium, quoted premium, taxes, payment schedule, accepted quote reference | Full comparison and approval history required |
| **Documents and approvals** | Renewal pack, declarations, accepted terms, maker, approver, insurer reference | Approval steps configurable |

**13\. High-Risk Screen Field Requirements**

**13.1 Login Screen**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Validation | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Policy number / corporate ID | Text | Yes | Recognized organization or deep-link prefill | Used to narrow tenant context |
| Registered email / mobile | Text | Yes | Must match active user identity | Supports email or mobile based login |
| Password | Password | Yes | Minimum 12 characters, mixed-case, digit, special character | Masked; password history enforced |
| OTP | 6-digit | Conditional | Valid for 5 minutes; resend cooldown | Mandatory for MFA or risk challenge |
| Remember / trust device | Checkbox | No | Only allowed on compliant devices | Not allowed for privileged exceptions |

**13.2 Add Member Wizard \- Step Set**

| Wizard step | Primary fields | Critical controls |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Step 1 \- Member master** | Member ID, name, DOB, gender, contact, IDs | Duplicate and identity validations |
| **Step 2 \- Employment / relationship** | DOJ, location, department, class, payroll or loan refs | Entry-date and class rules |
| **Step 3 \- Coverage** | Plan, benefit option, SA, riders, effective date, premium / contribution preview | Product and scheme rules |
| **Step 4 \- Nominee / beneficiary** | Nominee details, share %, contact, proof | Share total and documentary checks |
| **Step 5 \- Documents & declaration** | Upload checklist, declarations, consent | Upload type/size and declaration completeness |
| **Step 6 \- Review & submit** | Before-submit summary, validations passed, estimated outputs | Approvals triggered by threshold |

**13.3 Remove Member via LWD \- Detailed Fields**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Validation | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Termination type | Dropdown | Yes | Resignation / retirement / VRS / termination / death / other | Impacts premium and benefit logic |
| LWD | Date | Yes | Cannot predate join date; future LWD creates scheduled exit | Core coverage-boundary field |
| Coverage cease date | Derived date | Yes | Default \= LWD unless scheme differs | Read-only unless override rule exists |
| Refund premium eligible | Derived boolean | No | Depends on line and scheme | Read-only |
| Refund / fund impact | Currency / summary | No | Derived from pricing or fund service | Visible before approval |
| Supporting document | File upload | Conditional | Allowed types and scan status | Recommended for audit |

**13.4 Claim Initiation \- Detailed Fields**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Validation | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Member search | Search/picker | Yes | Must match insured register snapshot | Search by member ID, COI, name, DOB |
| Claim type | Dropdown | Yes | Allowed by product line | Death, disability, retirement, withdrawal, annuitant death, etc. |
| Date of event | Date | Yes | Cannot be future unless product explicitly supports scheduled event | Used for coverage-in-force check |
| Cause category | Dropdown | Conditional | Required for death/disability | Drives document checklist |
| Claimant / payee | Structured panel | Yes | Relationship, bank details, nominee / assignment checks | Mandatory for payout routing |
| Document checklist | Dynamic list | Yes | Varies by claim type, cause, scheme | No piece-meal request design target |
| Review and declaration | Checkbox \+ text | Yes | Declaration must be accepted before submit | Starts SLA clock |

**13.5 Renewal Screens \- Detailed Fields**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Validation | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Renewal period | Date range | Yes | Must align to annual renewal date and term | Read-only default with override by rule |
| Coverage options selected | Multi-select | Yes | Only configured benefits allowed | Carry-forward default permitted |
| Premium mode | Dropdown | Yes | Annual / half-yearly / quarterly / monthly if supported | Impacts schedule and display |
| GST treatment | Derived display | Yes | By product and current tax regime | Visible separately in commercial panels |
| Census file upload | File | Yes | Template, duplicates, DOB/DOJ/LWD validations | Immutable version after lock |
| Approval to lock census | Approval action | Yes | Maker-checker where configured | Lock required before quote acceptance |

**14\. Workflow Applicability by Product Type**

| Workflow | GTL | GCL | Savings / Gratuity | ULIP | Annuity / Pension |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Add / update / remove members** | Yes | Usually yes | Yes | Yes | Usually limited to scheme / annuitant cohort |
| **Claims / benefit requests** | Death and rider claims | Death / disability with lender split | Retirement / withdrawal / death-in-service | Benefit via unit / fund events | Annuity commencement / annuitant death / pension servicing |
| **Renewal processing** | Yes, annual | Often annual | Yes, valuation / annual cycle | Yes, with fund considerations | Contract-specific; less like pure annual renewal |
| **Fund / value views** | No | No | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Bank / payout servicing** | Claim-linked only | Claim-linked only | Benefit payout linked | Benefit payout linked | Core journey |
| **Certificate / COI** | Yes | Yes | Sometimes statement-based | Yes / statement-based | Less COI-focused; statement / advice heavy |

**15\. Validation Catalog**

| Validation ID | Rule |
| :---- | :---- |
| **VR-DATE-01** | Event / servicing date cannot be in future unless the workflow explicitly supports a scheduled future effective date. |
| **VR-DATE-02** | Effective date must fall within policy term, renewal windows, and scheme-defined entry / exit rules. |
| **VR-MEM-01** | Member ID must be unique within policy and scheme unless the action is an update against the same member key. |
| **VR-MEM-LWD** | LWD cannot predate join date; if future LWD is allowed, the termination remains scheduled until the LWD is reached. |
| **VR-CLAIM-01** | Claim cannot be submitted if cover was not in force on the event date unless the insurer override flow is triggered. |
| **VR-CLAIM-02** | Claim document checklist must be dynamically derived from claim type, cause, product, and scheme. |
| **VR-NOM-01** | Nominee / beneficiary share allocation must total 100% where shared entitlement is allowed. |
| **VR-AUTH-01** | Maker cannot approve own submission; approver must re-authenticate for sensitive actions. |
| **VR-FILE-01** | Allowed file types and size limits must be enforced; all uploads must be virus-scanned and encrypted at rest. |
| **VR-REN-01** | Renewal quote acceptance requires locked census version, visible commercial summary, and configured approval completion. |
| **VR-TAX-01** | Tax/GST calculation must be versioned and displayed separately from base premium or charge values. |
| **VR-PRIV-01** | Sensitive fields such as bank data, IDs, and medical info must be masked or hidden by role. |

**16\. Generic State Model**

| State | Meaning |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Draft** | Maker is preparing transaction; editable; not yet submitted |
| **Pending approval** | Awaiting checker / approver; maker may withdraw if allowed |
| **Pending insurer review** | Submitted to insurer / downstream ops; read-only to sponsor except comments |
| **Query raised** | Insurer has requested clarifications or missing items; sponsor must respond |
| **Resubmitted** | Sponsor responded to query; case returns to insurer review queue |
| **Approved** | Transaction accepted; downstream updates or documents triggered |
| **Rejected** | Transaction declined with reason codes and comments |
| **Completed** | All downstream outputs finished: PAS update, certificate, statement, or case closure |
| **Cancelled / withdrawn** | Draft or pre-review case withdrawn with reason and audit history |

**17\. Functional Requirements**

**17.1 Authentication and Access**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-AUTH-001** | The portal shall support email or mobile based login with password and MFA / OTP. |
| **FR-AUTH-002** | The portal shall support role-based access scopes by organization, policy, scheme, location, and action. |
| **FR-AUTH-003** | The portal shall support maker-checker configuration for claims, endorsements, billing, and renewals above configurable thresholds. |
| **FR-AUTH-004** | The portal shall support invitation-based onboarding, password reset, device trust, lockout, and session-expiry controls. |

**17.2 Dashboard and Navigation**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-DASH-001** | The portal shall provide a policy-aware portfolio dashboard with KPIs for lives, open claims, pending endorsements, renewals due, and billing items. |
| **FR-DASH-002** | The portal shall provide an action center for approvals, insurer queries, incomplete drafts, and SLA-risk tasks. |
| **FR-DASH-003** | The portal shall remember last-used policy context and allow quick context switching across assigned policies. |

**17.3 Member Administration**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-MEM-001** | The portal shall allow authorized users to add a member through a guided wizard and issue a submission acknowledgement. |
| **FR-MEM-002** | The portal shall support member update transactions with effective date, reason code, and before/after comparison. |
| **FR-MEM-003** | The portal shall support member removal using LWD and derive coverage-end treatment based on scheme rules. |
| **FR-MEM-004** | The portal shall not physically delete members; it shall move them to an exited, retired, or ceased state with full history. |
| **FR-MEM-005** | The portal shall support bulk member upload with row-level validation, partial acceptance, and annotated error download. |
| **FR-MEM-006** | The portal shall support nominee or beneficiary create, modify, and historical version display. |

**17.4 Endorsements**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-END-001** | The portal shall support endorsement types for member addition, member exit, member correction, coverage change, nominee update, rider change, and approved product-line specific changes. |
| **FR-END-002** | The portal shall display endorsement before/after comparison and financial or fund impact before submission. |
| **FR-END-003** | The portal shall support insurer query and sponsor response cycles without losing document history. |
| **FR-END-004** | The portal shall expose complete endorsement timeline, actors, status timestamps, and remarks. |

**17.5 Claims**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-CLM-001** | The portal shall support claim initiation for death, disability, retirement or withdrawal benefit, annuitant death, and other configured claim types. |
| **FR-CLM-002** | The portal shall perform coverage-in-force and eligibility pre-checks before allowing claim submission. |
| **FR-CLM-003** | The portal shall derive a dynamic document checklist by claim type, cause, product line, and scheme. |
| **FR-CLM-004** | The portal shall support claimant and payee capture including bank details, nomination, and authorization artifacts where applicable. |
| **FR-CLM-005** | The portal shall expose claim status, timeline, and outstanding actions end to end. |

**17.6 Renewals**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-REN-001** | The portal shall display all upcoming renewal windows with countdowns, readiness status, and tasks. |
| **FR-REN-002** | The portal shall support renewal assumptions, census upload, validation, census locking, quote review, and acceptance. |
| **FR-REN-003** | The portal shall support renewal commercial comparison including premium, taxes, charges, and change versus prior year. |
| **FR-REN-004** | The portal shall preserve renewal package versions, approvals, and accepted quote references. |

**17.7 Billing, Contributions, and Statements**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-BIL-001** | The portal shall show premium statements, due dates, taxes, and remittance references by policy. |
| **FR-BIL-002** | The portal shall support reconciliation views for expected vs received values and exception comments. |
| **FR-BIL-003** | The portal shall support fund, contribution, or value screens for ULIP, savings, gratuity, superannuation, annuity, and pension lines where relevant. |

**17.8 Documents, Reports, and Service**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-DOC-001** | The portal shall expose a document center for certificates, schedules, claim kits, statements, renewal packs, payout advice, and tax certificates. |
| **FR-DOC-002** | The portal shall retain document versions and download audit for sensitive outputs. |
| **FR-RPT-001** | The portal shall provide standard reports and policy-scoped export capability. |
| **FR-SVC-001** | The portal shall support service-request and grievance submission with status tracking. |

**17.9 Audit, Security, and Compliance**

| ID | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **FR-AUD-001** | The portal shall record immutable audit entries for logins, field changes, submissions, approvals, downloads, and status changes. |
| **FR-AUD-002** | The portal shall apply masking to sensitive fields by role and action. |
| **FR-AUD-003** | The portal shall retain executed rule or validation version references on high-risk transactions for replay and audit. |
| **FR-AUD-004** | The portal shall support evidence export for audit and dispute resolution. |

**18\. UX Requirements**

**•** Use policy-aware breadcrumbs and persistent policy context chips so users always know which contract is being serviced.

**•** Default to table \+ filter \+ drill-down patterns for operational screens; use wizard patterns for transactional journeys.

**•** Support save-as-draft on all multi-step journeys.

**•** Use explicit status badges with plain-language explanations; avoid opaque insurer/internal codes in the front-end.

**•** Allow bulk export of current filtered views with visible columns preserved.

**•** Use responsive layouts for laptop-first usage; mobile may be limited to member / annuitant self-service and low-complexity actions.

**•** Mask sensitive fields by default and reveal only through justified role-based interactions.

**•** Expose warnings before destructive or financially material actions, including exits, nominee changes, and quote acceptance.

**19\. Key Entities and Data Model**

| Entity | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Organization** | Master policyholder legal entity, addresses, contacts, tenant configuration |
| **Portal User** | Identity, roles, access scopes, MFA, device trust, preferences |
| **Policy** | Policy number, product line, dates, status, billing and renewal anchors |
| **Scheme / Plan** | Coverage structure, class rules, benefit options, product-specific settings |
| **Member / Annuitant** | Demographics, employment or source relationship, status, identifiers, bank/nominee references |
| **Coverage Record** | Benefit set, sum assured, rider set, effective dates, certificate references |
| **Endorsement Case** | Type, effective date, impacted entity, status, documents, approvals, decision trace |
| **Claim Case** | Claim type, event details, claimant/payee, documents, status, SLA timestamps |
| **Renewal Case** | Renewal window, census version, quote versions, accepted terms, approvals |
| **Statement / Contribution / Fund Record** | Premium, tax, remittance, units, fund value, payout history |
| **Document Artifact** | Type, version, source workflow, generation status, storage location, download history |
| **Audit Entry** | Actor, action, target object, before/after, timestamps, correlation ID |

**20\. Integration Architecture**

**•** Identity and access management for authentication, MFA, invitations, and role scope.

**•** PAS or policy core for policy, scheme, member, coverage, billing, and endorsement application.

**•** Claims core or workbench for claim case creation, case status, queries, and settlement outputs.

**•** Document generation / CCM for certificates, statements, renewal packs, and communications.

**•** Fund / accounting / payout services for ULIP, savings, gratuity, annuity, pension, and remittance visibility.

**•** Notification services for email, SMS, in-app alerts, and escalation reminders.

**•** File validation / malware scanning for uploaded documents and bulk files.

| Event | Direction | Meaning |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **member.add.requested** | Portal \-\> orchestration / PAS | New member request submitted |
| **member.remove.requested** | Portal \-\> orchestration / PAS | LWD-based exit requested |
| **member.batch.submitted** | Portal \-\> validation/orchestration | Bulk member file submitted |
| **endorsement.submitted** | Portal \-\> orchestration | Endorsement created and sent to workflow |
| **claim.intimated** | Portal \-\> claims core | Claim case opened |
| **renewal.census.locked** | Portal \-\> pricing / renewal orchestration | Renewal census frozen for quote |
| **renewal.accepted** | Portal \-\> policy / renewal orchestration | Quote approved and accepted |
| **document.generated** | CCM \-\> portal | Certificate / statement / pack available |
| **workflow.query.raised** | Insurer systems \-\> portal | Case query visible to sponsor |
| **workflow.completed** | Insurer systems \-\> portal | Case completed and artifacts ready |

**21\. Recommended API Surface**

| Endpoint | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- |
| **POST /auth/login** | Start login and MFA flow |
| **GET /policies** | List scoped policies |
| **GET /policies/{id}** | Get policy detail and extension tabs |
| **GET /members** | Search / filter member roster |
| **POST /members** | Create member request |
| **PATCH /members/{id}** | Create member update request |
| **POST /members/{id}/exit** | Submit LWD-based exit request |
| **POST /endorsements** | Create endorsement case |
| **GET /endorsements/{id}** | Get endorsement detail and timeline |
| **POST /claims** | Create claim case |
| **POST /claims/{id}/documents** | Upload claim documents |
| **GET /renewals** | List renewal windows |
| **POST /renewals/{id}/census** | Upload renewal census |
| **POST /renewals/{id}/accept** | Accept approved renewal quote |
| **GET /documents** | List document artifacts |
| **GET /audit** | Search audit log |

**22\. Documents and Communications**

| Artifact | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Certificate / COI** | Member cover confirmation or certificate artifact |
| **Policy schedule and benefit summary** | Policy-level coverage documentation |
| **Claim kit / claim acknowledgement** | Case opening and document expectations |
| **Renewal pack** | Renewal notice, quote, terms, census summary, declarations |
| **Premium statement / contribution statement** | Billing visibility and remittance reference |
| **Payout advice / annuity statement / tax certificate** | Retirement-line servicing outputs |
| **User invitation / approval / query notifications** | Operational communications |

**23\. Security, Privacy, and Compliance Requirements**

| Control area | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Strong authentication** | Password \+ MFA, step-up challenge for sensitive approvals, trusted-device controls |
| **Least-privilege authorization** | Role \+ permission \+ scope model by org, policy, scheme, location, and action |
| **Audit trail** | Immutable entries for every material change, submission, download, approval, and status transition |
| **Sensitive-data masking** | Bank data, identifiers, medical documents, and tax details masked by role |
| **Privacy controls** | Notice, consent capture where relevant, correction/update support, retention-aware erase requests |
| **Upload security** | Allowed file types, malware scanning, encryption at rest, secure object storage |
| **Operational logging** | Retain application and security logs as per insurer and applicable cyber guidance |
| **Documented status reasoning** | Every reject, return, or query must include visible reason codes or remarks |

**24\. Non-Functional Requirements**

| Area | Requirement |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Availability** | Target 99.9% monthly for portal front-end and critical APIs excluding planned maintenance |
| **Performance** | Dashboard load under 3 seconds for normal portfolios; filtered operational lists under 5 seconds for typical workloads |
| **Scalability** | Support large policy rosters and bulk census files without blocking single-record servicing journeys |
| **Resilience** | Retry-safe API design, idempotent workflow submission, resumable bulk processing, durable status records |
| **Accessibility** | Keyboard-navigable core flows, readable contrast, descriptive labels, usable form errors |
| **Localization** | Currency/date formatting, language packs, configurable text for scheme-specific labels and notices |
| **Observability** | Correlation IDs, workflow metrics, SLA breach alerts, queue-depth monitoring, download analytics |
| **Data retention** | Transaction and audit retention aligned to insurer, regulatory, and evidentiary needs |

**25\. Reporting and Analytics**

**•** Standard reports: active members, exited members, pending claims, claim ageing, endorsement ageing, renewal readiness, statement register, download register, audit extracts.

**•** Product-line reports: fund value summaries, annuity payout register, gratuity or superannuation exits, GCL borrower exposure, GST on billed premiums where applicable.

**•** Operational analytics: draft abandonment, workflow cycle time, query loops, approval latency, bulk-file error categories, top exception reasons.

**•** Governance analytics: user activity by role, SoD exceptions blocked, sensitive-document downloads, repeated failed logins, policy-wise service volumes.

**26\. Delivery Phasing**

| Phase | Recommended scope |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Phase 1A** | Authentication, dashboard, policy detail, member list/detail, add member, remove member via LWD, claim initiation, document center, notifications, user administration |
| **Phase 1B** | Update member, nominee / beneficiary changes, endorsement cockpit, statement list/detail, renewal dashboard, report exports, approval framework |
| **Phase 1C** | ULIP, savings, pension, and annuity specialized tabs; fund / payout views; existence-certificate workflow; advanced reconciliation; service-request integrations |
| **Phase 2** | Bulk APIs, broker view, deeper member / annuitant self-service, conversational assistance, analytics-led nudges, broader integrations |

**27\. Key Risks and Open Design Decisions**

**•** Whether claim initiation should be available to both the master policyholder and beneficiary / member, or only in sponsor-assisted mode.

**•** Whether member self-service and sponsor-admin self-service should share one app shell or two front-ends on the same back-end.

**•** Whether bulk endorsement upload belongs in Release 1 or should follow after single-record flows stabilize.

**•** Whether renewal acceptance requires digital signing integration or can remain a controlled click-through with document evidence.

**•** Whether ULIP fund-switch or redirection actions are in scope for the first release, given higher product-line complexity.

**•** How far billing visibility should go before a full accounting and collections integration is funded.

**28\. Executive Verdict**

The combined PRD is materially stronger than any one of the uploaded drafts on its own. It keeps the better portal-shell and screen modularity, adds deeper field-level rigor for the high-risk operational journeys, and extends the document into implementation territory through entity model, APIs, events, state handling, and compliance controls.

For engineering, the PRD is now specific enough to break into epics, services, APIs, and UI modules. For product and business, it is specific enough to validate scope, phase the build, and assess where the insurer wants strict self-service versus controlled insurer-assisted servicing.

**29\. Source Appendix**

| Source family | Reference set |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Uploaded references** | AXA Essential UC1 PRD v1.1; Group\_Life\_MPH\_Portal\_PRD\_v1; Group\_Life\_Member\_Portal\_PRD\_v1; Member Portal Requirements for Group Life Master Policyholder |
| **Official / market patterns used to validate the design** | IRDAI Master Circular on Life Insurance Products (2024); IRDAI Master Circular on Protection of Policyholders' Interests (2024); IRDAI Information and Cyber Security Guidelines (2023); IRDAI group life operational circular (2019); Government GST clarification on group term / group credit life; Sun Life plan sponsor services guide; LIC group customer / annuitant service pages; HDFC Life group service and claim surfaces |

**30\. Assessment Closure \- What Changed in v3.0**

The attached assessment concluded that the existing combined PRD was the correct engineering baseline, but still needed one more pass to become immediately executable. This version closes that gap. It keeps the stronger mechanics from the combined PRD \- split-lane bulk handling, LWD-as-event, visible regulatory timers, immutable audit, idempotent writes, and field-linked validation rules \- and deepens the architecture and delivery details that engineering teams need before sprint 1\.

| Assessment theme | Assessment observation | v3.0 closure |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Engineering baseline** | Use the combined execution-grade PRD as the core baseline, not the lighter business-summary version. | Retained the combined PRD as the primary design source and treated all new material as additive execution detail, not a rewrite. |
| **Bulk mechanics** | Split-lane processing and partial acceptance were correctly recognized as non-negotiable. | Explicitly carried the same pattern into member bulk upload, renewal census, and document-heavy workflows with queue, retry, and annotated-error expectations. |
| **Member exit model** | LWD must remain a coverage-cessation event, not a delete. | Expanded remove-member flows into explicit APIs, events, transition guards, and downstream financial/document side effects. |
| **Compliance as UX** | Claims SLA timers, GST display, and immutable register requirements must show up in the UI and workflow logic. | Added operational rules to API/error contracts, event traces, and go-live test cases so compliance is verified end-to-end rather than only described in prose. |
| **Integration detail** | The prior PRD needed stronger REST and event architecture boundaries. | Expanded service boundaries, canonical endpoints, event envelope rules, and consumer responsibilities so frontend, backend, and integration squads can start implementation immediately. |
| **Execution readiness** | Teams need more than requirements \- they need work packages, sprint order, test gates, and ownership. | Added team topology, epic map, sprint sequencing, definition-of-ready / definition-of-done, contract-test scope, and launch checklist. |

**31\. Execution-Ready Target Architecture**

The portal should be implemented as a policy-aware digital servicing layer with a thin presentation shell, a BFF/API gateway, domain-oriented services, and workflow/orchestration between the portal and insurer core systems. The portal must not become the system of record for policy, coverage, claims adjudication, or fund accounting. It owns user experience, intake contracts, approval choreography, denormalized read models, and audit-grade interaction history.

**31.1 Non-Negotiable Architecture Rules**

• Policy, member coverage, endorsement application, and financial truth stay in PAS/core systems. The portal owns interaction state, drafts, approvals, and read models \- not insurance truth.

• Every write command is correlation-ID-aware and idempotent. POST, PATCH, approve, accept, upload-complete, and resubmit actions must require an Idempotency-Key header.

• Maker-checker and scope checks are enforced server-side, never only in the UI.

• All business events are emitted through an outbox pattern and are replay-safe. Consumers must tolerate at-least-once delivery.

• Read traffic is served from denormalized query models where needed; write traffic always goes through workflow/orchestration and authoritative services.

• Bulk flows must support split-lane processing: valid rows progress; invalid rows are isolated with reason-coded remediation outputs.

• Audit history is immutable, append-only, tenant-scoped, actor-attributed, and linked to the exact rule/config versions used at decision time.

**31.2 Synchronous vs Asynchronous Interaction Model**

| Interaction | Mode | Why | System response expected in UI |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Login, MFA, policy switch, roster search, document search** | Synchronous | User is waiting and the action is lightweight/read-heavy. | Immediate success/failure with no workflow latency. |
| **Add member, update member, remove member via LWD, submit endorsement** | Sync acknowledgement \+ async processing | Need immediate request reference plus downstream PAS/workflow handling. | Return request reference, current status, next step, and timeline message within one call. |
| **Claim intimation submission** | Sync acknowledgement \+ async workflow | Front-end needs immediate acknowledgement; claims core processes later stages. | Return claim reference, initial status, checklist state, and SLA clocks. |
| **Bulk member file, bulk census file, bulk document parse** | Async job | Parsing/validation can be long-running and partial. | Return batch/job reference immediately; progress and partial outcomes are polled or pushed later. |
| **Document generation, certificate generation, statement generation** | Async event-driven | Rendering and delivery are side effects and should not block request acknowledgement. | UI shows pending/generated/failed with retry option if permitted. |
| **Notifications, reminders, escalation messages** | Async event-driven | Side-effect workload with external delivery dependencies. | Delivery state visible in communication history, not required in initial API response. |
| **Scheduled exports and analytics refresh** | Async batch | Not user-blocking and often large-volume. | User receives queued/export-ready status and downloadable artifact later. |

**32\. Service Boundaries and Systems of Record**

The cleanest implementation model is domain separation with explicit ownership. The front-end should never speak directly to every downstream system. A BFF/API gateway should provide a stable contract to the UI, while domain services and orchestrators mediate insurer-system complexity.

| Domain / module | Primary system of record | Service to build or harden now | Key notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Identity & access** | IAM / IdP | Portal Auth Adapter \+ Session Service | Invitation acceptance, MFA, trusted devices, policy/org scoping, and step-up re-auth for approvals. |
| **Policy context & portfolio read model** | PAS / policy core | Policy Query Service | Read-optimized portfolio, policy summary, benefit tabs, renewal markers, servicing eligibility flags. |
| **Member register & roster** | PAS for authoritative member state; portal read model for UX | Member Servicing Service | Member search, detail, eligibility flags, nomination summary, document status, audit links. |
| **Endorsement workflow** | Portal workflow \+ PAS for applied result | Endorsement Orchestration Service | Handles approval, effective dating, compare-before/after, PAS command dispatch, and completion callbacks. |
| **Claims intake** | Claims core / claims workbench | Claim Intake Adapter | Creates FNOL, passes documents and payee data, and mirrors statuses/timers back to portal. |
| **Renewal servicing** | Renewal engine / pricing / PAS | Renewal Service \+ Census Intake Job | Owns renewal calendar, census versioning, quote receipt, acceptance workflow, and issue handoff. |
| **Documents & communications** | CCM / DMS / object store | Document Facade | Certificate, statement, renewal pack, acknowledgement, query letters, payout advice, download audit. |
| **Billing / contribution / fund / payout visibility** | Accounting / fund admin / payout system | Financial Visibility Adapter | Read-only in early phases except remittance reference capture and supporting uploads. |
| **Notifications & tasks** | Workflow/task system \+ notification providers | Task and Notification Service | In-app inbox, email/SMS/WhatsApp adapters, due-date reminders, escalation rules. |
| **Audit, evidence, and compliance** | Immutable audit store | Audit Service | Before/after capture, actor attribution, rule version, policy scope, download audit, evidence export. |
| **Search, reporting, exports** | Portal reporting store | Reporting Service | Standard reports, exports, saved filters, scheduled deliveries, operational analytics. |
| **Configuration & catalogs** | Versioned config repository | Configuration Registry | Validation rules, role matrix, screen contract, reason codes, document checklist, product-line schema packs. |

**33\. Canonical API Contract Baseline**

The portal team should publish OpenAPI contracts before sprint 1 and treat them as product artifacts, not incidental engineering output. The following baseline is intentionally opinionated so frontend, backend, QA, and integration teams can align on a stable command/read model.

**33.1 Common Contract Rules**

• All mutating APIs require Authorization, X-Correlation-Id, and Idempotency-Key headers. Approval actions also require a step-up authentication assertion.

• Every success response for an asynchronous business action returns: requestRef, aggregateRef, status, correlationId, submittedAt, nextExpectedAction, and user-visible message.

• Validation failures return HTTP 422 with field-level codes. Business conflicts return HTTP 409 with reason code, current authoritative state, and safe next actions.

• SoD or scope failures return HTTP 403 and must include a business-readable explanation such as MAKER\_CHECKER\_VIOLATION or POLICY\_SCOPE\_DENIED.

• Every read response includes version metadata where the front-end may attempt conditional updates. Use ETag or explicit versionNo in payloads for optimistic concurrency.

• Uploads are two-step where practical: request upload slot \-\> upload file \-\> confirm upload complete. This avoids large-body retries on full business submissions.

**33.2 Members and Endorsements APIs**

| Endpoint | Purpose | Minimum contract elements | Success contract | Failure / side effects |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **GET /policies** | List scoped policies | query: orgId, productLine, status, renewalWindow; returns paging \+ filters | 200 with policy summaries and action eligibility flags | 403 if outside scope |
| **GET /policies/{policyId}** | Get policy detail and tab enablement | policyId path param | 200 with policy header, product tabs, permissions, counts, open tasks | 404 if not found or not visible |
| **GET /members** | Search / filter roster | policyId required; filters for status, class, location, query, pendingAction | 200 with paged roster and summary counts | 400 for invalid filter combos |
| **POST /members** | Create add-member request | policyId, schemeId, member identity, employment/source fields, cover inputs, consents, submissionMode | 202 with requestRef and initial state | 422 validation; 409 duplicate member or active-cover conflict; emits member.add.requested |
| **PATCH /members/{memberId}** | Create member-update request | versionNo, changed fields only, effectiveDate, reason, submissionMode | 202 with requestRef and comparison summary | 409 stale version; emits member.update.requested |
| **POST /members/{memberId}/exit** | Submit LWD-based exit request | lwd, exitReason, refundCalculationRequested, effectiveDate treatment, notes | 202 with requestRef, pending-exit or submitted state, financial-impact placeholder | 422 VR-DATE-LWD; 409 open workflow conflict; emits member.remove.requested |
| **POST /members/bulk** | Submit bulk member file | policyId, schemeId, fileRef, templateVersion, jobType(add/update/exit) | 202 with batchRef and parse status | File malware/type/template errors; emits member.batch.submitted |
| **POST /endorsements** | Create endorsement case | endorsementType, impacted entity refs, effectiveDate, payload, docs, submissionMode | 202 with endorsementRef and workflow state | 422 type-specific validation; emits endorsement.submitted |
| **GET /endorsements/{endorsementId}** | View endorsement detail/timeline | endorsementId | 200 with current/proposed values, approval chain, timeline, docs | 404/403 |
| **POST /approvals/{approvalId}/actions** | Approve / reject / send back | decision, remarks, stepUpAuthProof, optional query text | 200 with updated workflow state | 403 SoD violation; emits approval.actioned and downstream workflow event |

**33.3 Claims and Renewals APIs**

| Endpoint | Purpose | Minimum contract elements | Success contract | Failure / side effects |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **POST /claims** | Create FNOL / claim intimation | policyId, memberId, claimType, event block, claimant/payee, documents\[\], declarations, submissionMode | 202 with claimRef, initial status, checklist summary, SLA tracker anchors | 422 validation; 409 duplicate-claim block; emits claim.intimated |
| **GET /claims/{claimId}** | Get claim detail | claimId | 200 with timeline, checklist, query thread, payout state, SLA dates | 404/403 |
| **POST /claims/{claimId}/documents** | Upload or replace claim documents | uploadRef(s), documentType, remarks | 200/202 with updated document tracker state | 422 wrong doc type; emits claim.documents.received |
| **POST /claims/{claimId}/responses** | Respond to insurer query | message, docs if any, responseType | 202 with timeline update | 403 if state not query-raised; emits claim.query.responded |
| **GET /renewals** | List renewal windows | policyId optional, date range, status filters | 200 with renewal cards, tasks, quote expiry | 403/400 |
| **POST /renewals/{renewalId}/census** | Upload renewal census | fileRef, templateVersion, submissionMode | 202 with censusBatchRef and parse status | Template/file errors; emits renewal.census.submitted |
| **POST /renewals/{renewalId}/lock** | Freeze census for quoting | renewalId, censusVersion, declaration | 202 with locked status | 409 if open validation errors; emits renewal.census.locked |
| **POST /renewals/{renewalId}/accept** | Accept approved renewal quote | quoteVersion, approver declaration, stepUpAuthProof | 202 with acceptanceRef and issue-pending state | 409 quote expired or stale; emits renewal.accepted |

**33.4 Documents, Reporting, and Administration APIs**

| Endpoint | Purpose | Minimum contract elements | Success contract | Failure / side effects |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **GET /documents** | Search artifact library | filters: policyId, docType, subjectRef, date range, status | 200 paged list with masked metadata and download eligibility | 403 by scope |
| **POST /documents/{documentId}/download-intents** | Register a sensitive download and obtain secure link | reason or ticket if policy demands | 200 with signed URL / token and audit reference | 403/409 if blocked |
| **GET /reports/definitions** | List standard and saved reports | module, productLine, role context | 200 with report definitions and parameters | 403 |
| **POST /reports/runs** | Run export / report | definitionId, filters, outputFormat | 202 with export job ref | 422 invalid parameters; emits report.run.requested |
| **GET /tasks** | Retrieve inbox items | status, dueDate, type, policyId | 200 paged tasks and urgency badges | 403 |
| **GET /audit** | Search audit history | target type/id, actor, date range, action | 200 immutable entries and evidence links | 403 restricted by role |
| **POST /service-requests** | Raise general support/grievance request | category, subject, description, attachments | 202 with serviceRequestRef | 422 invalid category; emits service.requested |
| **POST /admin/users/invitations** | Invite or assign user | email/mobile, role, scope, expiry | 202 inviteRef | 403 admin-only; emits user.invited |

**33.5 Starter Error and Reason-Code Model**

| Code | Meaning | Typical trigger | Front-end behavior |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **VR-DATE-LWD** | Last Working Day invalid | LWD before join date, after policy expiry, or incompatible with effective date policy | Highlight LWD field, explain rule, block submission. |
| **MEMBER\_DUPLICATE\_ACTIVE** | Member already active under scope | National ID/member ref already has active cover | Show existing member reference and safe next actions. |
| **CLAIM\_DUPLICATE\_BLOCKED** | Potential duplicate claim | Same member \+ claim type \+ event date already exists | Block submit and deep-link to existing claim. |
| **QUOTE\_VERSION\_STALE** | Renewal quote is outdated | Accepted quote version not latest approved quote | Force refresh and re-review latest quote. |
| **MAKER\_CHECKER\_VIOLATION** | Approver cannot approve own submission | Same user or same delegated identity attempts approval | Block action and show required alternate approver. |
| **POLICY\_SCOPE\_DENIED** | User lacks policy/scheme scope | API call outside assigned scope | Hide action where possible; show authorization message if directly called. |
| **FILE\_TEMPLATE\_MISMATCH** | Uploaded file does not match expected template | Missing columns, wrong sheet, wrong version | Return annotated upload error and template download link. |

**34\. Event-Driven Architecture and Event Catalog**

All asynchronous workflow coordination should be described in AsyncAPI-style contracts and emitted from an outbox attached to the writing service. Every event envelope must include eventName, eventVersion, correlationId, causationId, tenantId, policyId or aggregate scope, aggregateType, aggregateId, actor, occurredAt, and idempotencyKey.

**34.1 Core Event Catalog**

| Event | Producer \-\> consumer(s) | When emitted | Key payload expectations |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **member.add.requested** | Portal member service \-\> workflow/PAS | Add-member submission accepted | policyId, schemeId, requestRef, member payload hash, actor, submission mode |
| **member.add.approved** | Workflow \-\> PAS / portal read model | Approval completed | requestRef, approvalRef, approved payload, effectiveDate |
| **member.add.completed** | PAS adapter \-\> portal | Authoritative create/apply successful | memberId, coverageRef, certificate trigger, applied version |
| **member.update.requested** | Portal member service \-\> workflow/PAS | Member correction/change submitted | memberId, changed fields, before/after hash, effectiveDate |
| **member.remove.requested** | Portal member service \-\> workflow/PAS | LWD exit submitted | memberId, lwd, exitReason, refund requested flag, open-claim indicator |
| **member.exit.effective** | PAS adapter \-\> billing/doc/read models | Exit applied as authoritative state | memberId, ceaseDate, premium impact, certificate/schedule delta |
| **member.batch.submitted** | Portal upload service \-\> validation job | Bulk file intake accepted | batchRef, fileRef, jobType, templateVersion |
| **member.batch.partiallyAccepted** | Validation/orchestration \-\> portal | Valid rows separated from error rows | batchRef, validRowCount, errorRowCount, errorFileRef |
| **endorsement.submitted** | Portal endorsement service \-\> workflow | Any endorsement case created | endorsementRef, type, impacted entities, docs, effectiveDate |
| **endorsement.completed** | PAS/workflow \-\> portal | Endorsement applied and closed | endorsementRef, applied changes, artifacts |
| **claim.intimated** | Portal claim service \-\> claims core | FNOL accepted | claimRef, memberId, claimType, event, payee summary, document inventory |
| **claim.query.raised** | Claims core \-\> portal/tasks/notifier | Insurer asks for clarification or documents | claimRef, query text, dueDate, requested docs |
| **claim.documents.received** | Portal \-\> claims core | Documents uploaded/reuploaded | claimRef, document metadata, uploader, document hash |
| **claim.approved** | Claims core \-\> portal/notifier | Claim approved for payout | claimRef, approved amount, payee, payout pending flag |
| **claim.paid** | Payout system \-\> portal | Payout completed | claimRef, payment date, UTR, paid amount |
| **renewal.census.submitted** | Portal renewal service \-\> validation job | Census upload accepted | renewalId, batchRef, fileRef, version |
| **renewal.census.locked** | Portal renewal service \-\> pricing/orchestration | Census frozen for quote | renewalId, censusVersion, lock actor, declaration |
| **renewal.quote.received** | Pricing/renewal core \-\> portal | Quote ready for review | renewalId, quoteVersion, premium summary, expiry |
| **renewal.accepted** | Portal renewal service \-\> PAS/issuance workflow | Approver accepted commercial terms | renewalId, quoteVersion, approver, acceptanceRef |
| **document.generated** | CCM/document service \-\> portal | Artifact became available | documentId, type, subjectRef, version, secure location |
| **notification.dispatch.requested** | Domain services \-\> notifier | User or ops communication required | templateId, channel, recipient refs, policy context |
| **workflow.query.raised** | Workflow/insurer ops \-\> portal | Non-claim query or send-back raised | target aggregate, text, due date, required responder role |
| **workflow.completed** | Workflow/orchestrator \-\> portal | Case closed | aggregateRef, final state, final artifacts, timestamps |

**34.2 Event Handling Rules**

• Partition keys should be chosen per aggregate: memberId for member events, claimRef for claim events, endorsementRef for endorsement events, renewalId for renewal events, and batchRef for bulk jobs.

• All consumers must be idempotent and maintain a de-duplication window using eventId or idempotencyKey.

• Failures after local transaction commit must be recovered by outbox replay, not by re-submitting the user command.

• DLQ routing is mandatory for non-transient consumer failures, with replay tooling and visible operational metrics.

• Portal UI should never infer final completion solely from local write success; it must wait for completion events or authoritative status refresh.

**35\. Entity-Specific State Models and Transition Guards**

The assessment correctly called out that one generic Draft \-\> Pending \-\> Approved model is not sufficient. Each business aggregate needs its own state machine and transition guards because claims, members, endorsements, renewals, and bulk jobs behave differently.

**35.1 Member Register / Coverage Request States**

| State | Next state(s) | Transition trigger | Guard / note |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Draft** | Pending Approval / Submitted | User saves or submits | Draft is portal-owned and editable by maker. |
| **Pending Approval** | Submitted / Rejected / Cancelled | Approver action | SoD and step-up auth required when configured. |
| **Submitted** | Active / Pending Exit / Rejected / Query Raised | Workflow \+ PAS evaluation | Member create/update command dispatched to authoritative system. |
| **Active** | Pending Exit / Deceased / Reinstated(change) / Updated | Servicing action | Cannot transition to Exited without valid LWD/cease treatment. |
| **Pending Exit** | Exited / Rejected / Cancelled | Future-dated exit or pending insurer action | Open claims warning required; cease date derived per scheme rules. |
| **Exited** | Reinstated / Closed | Rehire/reinstate or final archive | Historical record remains immutable and queryable. |
| **Rejected** | Draft / Closed | User revises or case closes | Reject reason mandatory. |

**35.2 Claim Case States**

| State | Next state(s) | Transition trigger | Guard / note |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Draft** | Submitted / Cancelled | Claimant or MPH submits | Duplicate-claim checks run before submission. |
| **Submitted** | Documents Pending / Under Assessment / Query Raised | Claims core intake outcome | 15-day document-query clock starts. |
| **Documents Pending** | Under Assessment / Query Raised / Rejected | Checklist completion or insufficiency | Checklist must not be dripped piecemeal after initial query window without compliant reason. |
| **Under Assessment** | Investigation / Approved / Rejected / Query Raised | Claims ops evaluation | Medical/legal/investigation flags may route separately. |
